title: The Strait That Isn't: How Iranian 'Shipping Management' Collapsed Real Traffic by 90%+ While Claiming Control
content:
## The Strait That Isn't

While Iranian officials insist they're "managing shipping" through the Strait of Hormuz, the data tells a different story — one of near-total commercial collapse.

AIS satellite data shows shipping traffic has collapsed 87 to 97 percent since February 2026. The waterway that normally sees roughly 175 daily transits is now down to a handful. The UK Maritime Trade Operations office confirms "extremely limited" commercial traffic — just three confirmed transits. Sixty-two vessels have been identified transmitting false AIS positions, masking their movements entirely.

Tankers have been attacked in May 2026. The US Navy engaged Iranian tankers on May 8th. Insurance premiums for anything still attempting Hormuz passage have reportedly spiked more than 400 percent.

The official line from Tehran: everything is functioning normally under orderly Iranian oversight. The US says the strait remains "open" — while simultaneously rushing additional carrier groups to the region.

That's the verbal hedge. The operational reality is a waterway that has effectively closed to commercial traffic while two governments insist otherwise.

**What the data shows:**
- 87–97% collapse in shipping transits since February 2026
- ~175 daily transits → 6–22 per day
- 62 vessels using AIS spoofing to disguise position
- UK Maritime Trade Operations: "extremely limited" commercial traffic
- 3 confirmed transits via official channels
- Insurance premiums up 400%+ for remaining traffic

The gap between the press release and the radar return is the whole story.
